name: savecompat-erase
description: Reaches the global save-compatibility gate for a non-CURRENT SRAM image, dismisses the diagnostic, moves the cursor to the only destructive control ("Erase All Save Data"), selects it (Yes/No confirmation shown), then confirms "Yes". The final checkpoint must show a changed sram_hash (whole-chip wipe + fresh CURRENT initialization) and the normal save menu reached. The freshly-initialized CURRENT image embeds this build's live FE8_EXPANSION_BUILD_COMMIT into ExpansionSaveMeta.buildCommitShort (include/save_format.h offset 0x24, 9 bytes) and its dependent checksum (offset 0x2E, 2 bytes) -- intentionally variable across every commit -- so every checkpoint's sram_hash_exclude_ranges excludes exactly those two byte ranges (absolute SRAM offsets 29640..29648 and 29650..29651), keeping every checkpoint in this scenario in the same normalized hash space (the pre-erase checkpoints have no metadata record present at all, so excluding these bytes there is a no-op, never masking real corruption) per docs/save_format.md's normalized-hash policy.
